name: Senso-ji Temple
description: Tokyo's oldest and most iconic temple, approached through the Nakamise shopping street. Go early morning to see it in near-solitude before the crowds arrive.

name: Shibuya Crossing
description: The world's busiest pedestrian crossing — best experienced from the Starbucks or Mag's Park overlook. In the evening rush it feels almost choreographed.

name: Tsukiji Outer Market
description: The vendors and sushi stalls that spilled out from the old wholesale market. Tuna skewers, tamagoyaki, fresh oysters — one of the best breakfast walks anywhere.

name: Meiji Shrine
description: A forested Shinto shrine in the heart of the city — the walk through the towering torii gate and camphor forest is a genuine decompression from urban Tokyo.

name: teamLab Planets
description: Walk barefoot through rooms of infinite digital flowers, light forests, and a room that's entirely mirrors and koi fish. Immersive doesn't begin to cover it. Book ahead.

name: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
description: Three distinct gardens — French formal, English landscape, Japanese traditional — across 58 beautiful hectares. In spring the cherry blossoms here are among Tokyo's finest.

name: Akihabara Electric Town
description: Multi-story electronics shops, anime figure stores, maid cafés, and retro game arcades in every direction. Even if none of that is your thing, it's unlike anywhere else.

name: Harajuku Takeshita Street
description: Tokyo's youth fashion epicentre — crepe stands, costume shops, and street styles you genuinely won't find anywhere else. Weekends are absolute controlled chaos.

name: Tokyo National Museum
description: Japan's oldest and largest museum — samurai armour, Buddhist sculpture, ukiyo-e prints, tea ceremony objects. More than 110,000 items; pick one or two buildings and go deep.

name: Odaiba Waterfront
description: An artificial island in Tokyo Bay with a miniature Statue of Liberty, the Rainbow Bridge, and the best skyline view of the city — especially photogenic at night.

name: Yanaka Old Town Walk
description: The neighbourhood that survived the war and earthquakes — narrow shopping streets, old wooden temples, cats everywhere. Feels like stepping back fifty years.

name: Sushi Saito (Roppongi area)
description: One of the most coveted sushi counters in Japan — 12 seats, Edomae-style, each piece arriving at exactly the right temperature. Near-impossible reservations, transcendent food.

name: Ichiran Ramen Shibuya
address: B1F Beam Bldg, 31-2 Udagawacho, Shibuya, Tokyo
description: Solo ramen dining in individual booths — you customise every element (broth richness, noodle texture, garlic level) on a form. Introvert's paradise, genuinely great tonkotsu.

name: Torishiki Yakitori
description: Michelin-starred yakitori counter — Yoshiteru Ikegawa grills every skewer himself, from chicken heart to tsukune. Counter seats only; reserve well ahead.

name: Depachika at Isetan Shinjuku
description: The basement food hall of Japan's most famous department store — bento boxes, wagashi sweets, sashimi counters, pastries from Tokyo's top bakeries. An underrated Tokyo ritual.

name: Ryogoku Sumo Stadium
description: Tournament weeks (January, May, September) let you watch sumo all day — get cheap upper seats and move down as matches get better. Even the practice (keiko) sessions are fascinating.

name: Tokyo Skytree
description: The world's tallest tower at 634 metres — the view from the Tembo Deck at 350m covers the entire Kanto plain on a clear day, and all the way to Mount Fuji.

name: Nezu Shrine
description: Tokyo's answer to Kyoto's Fushimi Inari — a long row of red torii gates through forested grounds. Far less visited than it deserves to be.

name: Shibuya Stream Rooftop & Scramble Square
description: The Scramble Square observation deck at 229m directly above the famous crossing — the best urban viewpoint in Tokyo, especially as the city lights up at dusk.

name: Koenji Retro Shopping Street
description: Tokyo's bohemian neighbourhood — vintage clothing shops, record stores, live jazz bars, and ramen joints without the tourist markup. A locals' evening favourite.

name: Mount Takao Day Trip
description: An hour from Shinjuku on the Keio line, Mt Takao offers forest trails, a monkey park, and a free-range cable car. On clear winter days you can see Fuji from the summit.

name: Nakameguro Canal Walk
description: The tree-lined Meguro River with independent coffee roasters, vintage boutiques, and tiny restaurants. In spring it becomes Tokyo's most photogenic cherry blossom tunnel.

name: Roppongi Art Triangle
description: Mori Art Museum, National Art Center, and Suntory Museum of Art form a walkable triangle. The Mori in Roppongi Hills also has the city's best 360-degree view deck.

name: Hamarikyu Gardens
description: A tidal garden at the edge of Tokyo Bay — the teahouse on the pond serves matcha with a wagashi sweet. The skyscrapers framing the old willow trees are a perfect Tokyo contrast.

name: Shimokitazawa Music Scene
description: Tokyo's live music heartland — cramped basement venues, vinyl record shops, and theatre bars in every direction. The best neighbourhood in the city for a late night out.

name: Tofu Restaurant Ukai Tofuya
description: Traditional kaiseki-style tofu cuisine in a series of tranquil tatami rooms with a Japanese garden. Right below Tokyo Tower. One of those once-in-a-trip experiences.
